entities.name: Apartment remains rent stabilized
entities.description: Respondents challenge the landlord's claim that the apartment was lawfully deregulated in 2008 and assert it remains subject to rent stabilization.

relief_sought: Declaratory relief that apartment is rent stabilized

entities.name: Rent overcharge counterclaim
entities.description: Respondents counterclaim that they have been charged rent in excess of the lawful stabilized rent.

entities.name: Petition alleging high rent vacancy decontrol
entities.description: The landlord's petition states the premises are not subject to rent stabilization due to high rent vacancy decontrol, claiming lawful deregulation in 2008.
entities.source_quote: Paragraph six of the petition also alleges '[t]he premises are not subject to the Rent Stabilization Code by virtue of high rent vacancy decontrol.'

entities.name: Landlord's calculation of improvements
entities.description: Petitioner claims it used lawfully permitted guideline increases, vacancy increases, and improvements totaling less than $200, requiring $8,000 in actual improvements.
entities.source_quote: Petitioner insists it availed itself of lawfully permitted guideline increases, vacancy increases, and improvements totaling less than $200. For an increase of $200 to the legal regulated rent, petitioner needed to show $8,000 in actual improvements made to the apartment (1/40 of $8,000 is $200).

entities.name: Respondents' discovery requests for renovation documents
entities.description: Respondents sought documents relevant to actual or planned 'renovation or construction work beyond ordinary maintenance or repair' to challenge the deregulation claim.
entities.source_quote: Respondents sought documents relevant to the actual or planned 'renovation or construction work beyond ordinary maintenance or repair' as part of their discovery requests.

entities.name: Summary non-payment proceeding
entities.description: Landlord's court action to recover unpaid rent and potentially evict tenants for non-payment.

steps: Alleges $45,900 in unpaid rent from February 2017 through April 2019

entities.name: Verified answer with counterclaims
entities.description: Tenants' formal response to the petition asserting defenses and making counterclaims against the landlord.

steps: Includes defense that apartment remains rent stabilized

entities.name: Discovery motion practice
entities.description: Legal process where parties request and exchange relevant documents and information before trial.

entities.name: Discovery granted to respondents
entities.description: Court granted respondents' request for discovery regarding renovation/construction work relevant to deregulation claim.

entities.name: Rent Stabilization Code
entities.description: Governs rent-stabilized apartments in New York City, including regulations on rent increases, improvements, and deregulation.

entities.name: High Rent Vacancy Decontrol
entities.description: Provision allowing deregulation of rent-stabilized apartments when vacant and legal rent reaches statutory threshold.

entities.name: Civil Practice Law and Rules § 2219(a)
entities.description: Requires recitation of papers considered in review of a motion.

entities.name: Nonpayment of Rent
entities.description: Landlord's claim that tenant failed to pay rent, leading to a nonpayment proceeding.

entities.name: Residential Lease dated December 1, 2014
entities.description: Establishes the contractual rent amount and terms of tenancy between petitioner and respondents.
entities.source_quote: a copy ofa residential lease dated December 1, 2014, between petitioner and respondents, which reflected a monthly rent of$1700

entities.name: Rent Ledger
entities.description: Document showing the amount of rent allegedly owed by the respondents through a specific date.
entities.source_quote: a rent leger reflecting $137,700 owed through March of 2025

entities.name: DHCR Registrations
entities.description: Official records showing prior tenancies and the landlord's claim of exemption from rent regulation for the subject premises. Contains conflicting apartment designations.
entities.source_quote: copies ofDHCR registrations which reflect prior tenancies, and the period petitioner deemed the subject premises exempt from rent regulation

entities.name: Prior Leases (760 Liberty Realty Corp.)
entities.description: Leases for previous tenants showing the rental history and increases, including a reference to an Individual Apartment Increase (IAI).

entities.description: Written statement indicating no documents exist regarding a 2005 Individual Apartment Improvement (IAI) for $200, despite its existence being acknowledged.
entities.source_quote: Petitioner's December 5,2024, written response to the demand for documents relevant to the individual apartment improvements states "[n]o documents responsive to this demand still exist notwithstanding there was an findividual Apartment Increasel because ("IAI") for $200.00 in 2005."

entities.name: Housing Court Nonpayment Proceeding
entities.description: A summary proceeding initiated by a landlord in Housing Court to recover possession of real property and a judgment for unpaid rent.

steps: Filing of Petition and Notice of Petition

entities.name: Court Denies Motion for Directed Verdict
entities.description: The court initially denied the respondent's oral motion for a directed verdict based on failure to state a cause of action, allowing the trial to proceed.

entities.name: Court Revisits Motion to Dismiss
entities.description: After cross-examination and during respondent's direct examination, the court elected to revisit the motion to dismiss and allowed counsel to submit briefs on the issue.
